scenario_title: Delete non-apex RRset that is already absent after prerequisites succeed
test_rationale: The zone contains a name that is definitively in use via an A rrset, while its MX rrset is absent. The UPDATE first satisfies a prerequisite on the name and absence of the MX rrset, then issues delete_rrset for that already-absent MX rrset. This isolates the protocol gap to the UPDATE reply: both servers should leave post-state unchanged, but they may differ on whether the operation is accepted or rejected when nothing exists to delete.

group_1.behavior: Treats the delete of an already-absent rrset as a successful no-op after prerequisites pass.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the operation-phase delete of an absent rrset even though the prerequisite was satisfied.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 requires prerequisites to be checked before update operations, and §3.4.2.7 defines delete_rrset as deleting all RRs of a given name/type, but it does not say whether deleting an rrset that is already absent must succeed as a no-op or fail once the operation phase is reached. That leaves the reply code for this state-dependent case unspecified.
